title: Vetiver and sandalwood room spray
subtitle: A deep, grounding room fragrance spray with vetiver, sandalwood, and bergamot essential oils
excerpt: Vetiver and sandalwood are both heavy base notes with slow-releasing, earthy and woody characters. Bergamot at 15% of the essential oil blend lifts the heaviness and adds a citrus brightness without dominating. The combination gives a calm, sophisticated room fragrance that develops over a few minutes after spraying. Yield is 200 ml.
sourceNotes: Vetiver EO (Vetiveria zizanioides, steam-distilled root): one of the heaviest, most viscous essential oils; deep, earthy, smoky, and woody. Very slow-releasing; provides excellent longevity in a room spray. Sandalwood EO: use sustainably sourced Santalum album or Santalum spicatum; Amyris balsamifera is a more affordable alternative. Bergamot EO (Citrus bergamia): phototoxic on skin; safe in a room spray not applied to skin. Room spray mechanics: isopropyl alcohol dissolves the essential oils and acts as a solubiliser and preservative. Vetiver is thick and may be slow to dissolve; warming the alcohol very slightly (not above 40°C) helps.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One spray bottle per batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Allow the scent to develop on the first spray; the base notes emerge over a few minutes.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed spray bottle. The isopropyl alcohol preserves the blend. Base notes such as vetiver and sandalwood have excellent longevity in a blend.

glossaryTerms.definition: The heaviest, slowest-evaporating component in a fragrance blend. Base notes like vetiver and sandalwood have large, complex molecules that volatilise slowly, giving them longevity. They are often barely perceptible immediately on application and develop over several minutes as lighter notes fade.

content.text: : slow to evaporate, often barely noticeable at first, building over several minutes. This spray opens with bergamot; vetiver and sandalwood follow as the bergamot fades, giving the blend depth. Vetiver is one of the thickest essential oils; it dissolves fully in isopropyl alcohol but takes a moment of stirring. Shake before each use. Bergamot is phototoxic on skin; this spray is for rooms only.

items.prepNote: 90%+ grade; dissolves and suspends the essential oils
Deep, earthy base note; dissolve in the alcohol first
Creamy, woody base note; sustainably sourced
0.5% of total; lifts the blend and provides a bright opening note

content.text: Measure the isopropyl alcohol into a small jug or directly into the spray bottle. Add the vetiver, sandalwood, and bergamot essential oils to the alcohol and stir until fully dissolved; vetiver is thick and may take a moment. Add the distilled water and stir or shake to combine. Pour into the spray bottle if mixing in a jug. Label with the batch date.

Shake before use. Spray 3 to 4 pumps into the centre of a room or towards the ceiling. Allow 2 to 3 minutes for the full fragrance profile to develop as the bergamot opens and the vetiver and sandalwood follow. Do not spray on skin, fabrics, or open flames.

Store in a sealed spray bottle away from heat and direct sunlight. Keeps 12 months. Base notes such as vetiver have excellent longevity in a blend and the fragrance should remain strong throughout the shelf life.
